dossier.bio: Ms. Lin Chen — a returning guest to Rosewood Hong Kong, an Affluential Explorer with a quiet, ritual-driven rhythm and a discerning palate.
conversationHooks: Third stay with us at Victoria Dockside — she has previously praised Henry's tasting menu and the suite's sandalwood scent (CRM, Oct 2025).
She gravitates to early-morning movement and valued the Asaya wellness space during her recent stay (CRM, Feb 2026).
handleWithCare: Public-profile claims in the CRM (role, recent events) could not be independently verified via open web search on 2026-05-16; treat as internal context only and do not reference unprompted.
Declined alcohol throughout her Phuket stay — offer non-alcoholic pairings by default and without commentary.
Prefers silence and quiet; do not stage music or unsolicited in-room audio.

suppressed.reason: Behavioral detail (Wong Kar-wai interest) feels overly tracked; preserve in itinerary reasoning only, not conversationHooks
6 AM beach walks + Asaya twice detail is surveillance-adjacent; keep pattern-level insight, drop specificity
Phuket stay reference strips non-HK context; focus on Victoria Dockside only in conversation layer

reasoning: Prior HK stay (Oct 2025): thermostat noted at 19°C; matches stated preference roomTempC=19.
Prior HK stay (Oct 2025): praised the suite's sandalwood scent — keep low, per property default register.
Prior HK stay (Feb 2026): requested down-free pillows — extend to bedding by default.
Stated preference: audio = silence; suppress in-room music and welcome playlist.
Property default lighting (ambient) retained, dimmed to match quiet register.

welcomeAmenity.name: Tea flight — three estates
welcomeAmenity.story: Curated by Lock Cha — Hong Kong's most respected tea master
welcomeAmenity.reasoning: Pescatarian-compatible and alcohol-free, matching her stated pattern of declining alcohol. Builds on her interest in cultural Hong Kong and her morning-ritual rhythm. Sets up a possible Placemaker introduction to Selena Lai for a Wing Lee Street tea ceremony later in the stay — a Relationship Hospitality thread, not a transaction.

itinerary.title: Sunrise Tai Chi on the harbour terrace
itinerary.whyHere: Victoria Harbour view; Asaya signature ritual
itinerary.reasoning: She valued Asaya during her recent stay and demonstrates a preference for early-morning wellness — a signature rhythm we can honour at Victoria Dockside.

itinerary.title: Wing Lee Street tea ceremony
itinerary.whyHere: Preserved 1950s street; a Lock Cha private tea ceremony
itinerary.reasoning: Pairs with the Lock Cha welcome tea flight and her expressed curiosity about heritage Hong Kong; a Placemaker moment with Selena Lai, third-generation Lock Cha master.

itinerary.title: Wong Kar-wai film locations walk
itinerary.whyHere: Mong Kok / Central in WKW's frames — a guest-requested past favourite
itinerary.vendorOrPlace: Hong Kong Island and Kowloon, guided
itinerary.reasoning: She expressed interest in Hong Kong's cinematic heritage during her prior stay. Offering the walk honours a conversation she opened with us.

itinerary.title: Indian tasting at Chaat
itinerary.reasoning: Pescatarian-friendly with a Placemaker chef (Manav Tuli) in-house. She has shown appreciation for tasting menus at Victoria Dockside; Chaat offers a different culinary register for a returning guest seeking discovery.
